an5.191:1.1: “Monks, these five ancient brahmanical traditions are now observed among dogs but not among brahmans.
an5.191:1.3: “In the past, brahman males mated only with brahman females and not with non-brahman females.
an5.191:1.4: At present, brahman males mate with brahman females and with non-brahman females.
an5.191:1.5: At present, male dogs mate only with female dogs and not with female non-dogs.
an5.191:1.6: This is the first ancient brahmanical tradition that is now observed among dogs but not among brahmans.
an5.191:2.1: “In the past, brahman males mated with brahman females only in-season and not out-of-season.
an5.191:2.2: At present, brahman males mate with brahman females in-season and out-of-season.
an5.191:2.3: At present, male dogs mate with female dogs only in-season and not out-of-season.
an5.191:2.4: This is the second ancient brahmanical tradition that is now observed among dogs but not among brahmans.
an5.191:3.1: “In the past, brahman males did not buy or sell brahman females, but took up cohabitation for the sake of reproduction simply through mutual attraction.
an5.191:3.2: At present, brahman males buy and sell brahman females, and take up cohabitation for the sake of reproduction simply through mutual attraction.
an5.191:3.3: At present, male dogs do not buy or sell female dogs, but take up cohabitation for the sake of reproduction simply through mutual attraction.
an5.191:3.4: This is the third ancient brahmanical tradition that is now observed among dogs but not among brahmans.
an5.191:4.1: “In the past, brahmans did not make a stash of wealth, grain, silver, or gold.
an5.191:4.2: At present, brahmans make stashes of wealth, grain, silver, & gold.
an5.191:4.3: At present, dogs do not make a stash of wealth, grain, silver, or gold.
an5.191:4.4: This is the fourth ancient brahmanical tradition that is now observed among dogs but not among brahmans.
an5.191:5.1: “In the past, brahmans searched for alms for their morning meal in the morning, and for their evening meal in the evening.
an5.191:5.2: At present, brahmans, having eaten as much as they like, swelling their bellies, leave taking the leftovers.
an5.191:5.3: At present, dogs search for alms for their morning meal in the morning, and for their evening meal in the evening.
an5.191:5.4: This is the fifth ancient brahmanical tradition that is now observed among dogs but not among brahmans.
an5.191:5.5: “These, monks, are the five ancient brahmanical traditions that are now observed among dogs but not among brahmans.”
